No direct verification found. The fluorescence_colocalization task metadata indicates the Fluorescent Cells sample contains approximately 3-6 cells. The min_expected_objects value of 3 may refer to the minimum expected overlapping particles after boolean AND operation between red and green channel masks. The actual count would depend on the threshold method used and the specific overlap characteristics of the red and green fluorescent signals in each cell.

No authoritative source found to verify this exact value. The verifier.py logic does not enforce this upper bound. The value appears to be a reasonable estimate for the maximum number of discrete overlapping fluorescent particles that could result from analyzing 3-6 cells with boolean overlap analysis, but the actual count would depend on the fluorescent marker distribution and threshold settings.

privileged_info_summary: The task performs boolean overlap analysis on the Fluorescent Cells sample image from Fiji/ImageJ. Output file paths are verified across task description, setup scripts, export scripts, and verifier code: red channel mask at /home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/mask_red.tif, green channel mask at /home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/mask_green.tif, overlap mask at /home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/mask_overlap.tif, and object counts saved to /home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/overlap_counts.csv. The Fluorescent Cells sample is a real, built-in Fiji/ImageJ sample image (not synthetic) containing approximately 3-6 cells with red (rhodamine) and green (FITC) fluorescent markers. Expected object counts (minimum 3, maximum 50) refer to discrete overlapping particles found in the intersection mask after applying binary thresholding and logical AND operations on individual channel masks.
